Question: What is EOH?
Answer: EOH is an annual 2-day event that showcases engineering at UIUC, a tradition that has been evolving for over a century. As the largest student-run STEM fair in the country, EOH presents over 200 exhibits to approximately 35,000 visitors each year. Other key events include a Tesla Coil Concert, Build-a-Blinkie, a Keynote Speaker (past speakers include Yu Pan (YouTube, PayPal) and Todd Little (Oracle)), middle and high school design competitions, and more. Visit us on April 10 and 11, 2026!

Question: What are the event hours?
Answer: The shuttle runs from 8:00 am to 5:30 pm on both Friday, April 10, and Saturday, April 11. Exhibits are open from 9:00 am to 4:00 pm unless otherwise noted due to weather.

Question: Where can I find help or ask questions during the event?
Answer: Visit one of our Visitor’s Booths located at the Campus Instructional Facility (CIF), Graziano Plaza, outside Sidney Lu Mechanical Engineering Building, or outside the Electrical and Computer Engineering Building (ECEB).

Question: What should I do in case of severe weather?
Answer: Check the event website for updates on where exhibits will be held: https://eohillinois.org

Question: Are minors allowed to attend alone?
Answer: No. All minors must be accompanied by a parent or guardian at all times.

Question: Do you have events suitable for elementary, middle, and high schoolers?
Answer: Yes! EOH is open to everyone and we have activities suitable for all ages.

Question: Is EOH free? Do I need to register?
Answer: EOH is completely free and open to everyone. No registration is required—just show up on Friday and/or Saturday to check out the exhibits.

Question: Where can I find information about specific exhibits?
Answer: Check out the Visitor’s Guide for a comprehensive list of all exhibits, locations, special events, parking, shuttle info, emergency details, and maps. A PDF is available on https://eohillinois.org or physical copies can be picked up at Visitor’s Booths.

Question: Will food be available on campus during the event?
Answer: Yes! Food trucks will be located on Mathews Avenue.

Question: Which food trucks will be available?
Answer: Available trucks include Juanito’s Tacos, Chicago Grill, La Paloma, Burrito King, Pastamania, Brien’s Bistro, Smith’s Burger Co., Kona Ice, Travelin’ Tom’s Coffee Truck, Urban Oasis, and Stango Cuisine.

Question: Where can I park on Friday?
Answer: Lot E-14 (near State Farm Center) offers all-day parking with shuttle service. Lot B-4 (North Campus) offers all-day parking without a shuttle; visitors can walk to Bardeen Quad or use the nearest shuttle stop at B-22.

Question: Where can I park on Saturday?
Answer: Available lots include B1, B17, C09, D09, E14, F23, F28, B4, and F29 across campus.

Question: Is shuttle service available?
Answer: Yes. Shuttles run from 8:00 am to 5:30 pm on both days, stopping at major parking lots and exhibit hubs such as ECEB, CIF, and Everitt Laboratory. Full routes are available in the Visitor’s Guide.

Question: What are the shuttle stops?
Answer: Stops include Lot E-14, Everitt Laboratory, Campus Instructional Facility (CIF), Electrical and Computer Engineering Building (ECEB), Lot B-22, and Goodwin & Springfield.

Question: Where do buses pick up and drop off?
Answer: All buses must use Lot B-22. Visitors should walk or take the shuttle to B-22 for bus pickup at the end of the day.

Question: Are there any street closures?
Answer: Yes. From 7:00 am to 6:00 pm, W. Springfield Ave (Wright to Mathews) and S. Mathews St (Green to Springfield) will be closed to all but authorized vehicles.

Question: What is the protocol for a lost or missing child?
Answer: Immediately notify the nearest Visitor’s Booth. A volunteer will bring the child to the Visitor’s Booth in CIF and notify 911. A parent or guardian can meet the child at CIF.

Question: What should I do in case of a medical emergency?
Answer: Notify a nearby volunteer immediately and go to the EMS tent on Graziano Plaza, located between Engineering Hall and Everitt Laboratory.

Question: Which hospitals are nearby?
Answer: Nearby hospitals include Carle Foundation Hospital and OSF Presence Covenant Medical Center.

Question: Is there a Lost & Found?
Answer: Yes. Lost items can be taken to the Visitor’s Booth in CIF (9:00 am–5:00 pm, Friday & Saturday) or to the Engineering Council Office (Engineering Hall 103C) at other times.

Question: How can I identify staff and volunteers?
Answer: Shirt colors indicate roles: Volunteers (Yellow), Exhibitors (Light Pink), Committee Members (White), Sponsors (Latte), Judges (Navy), HSDC (Bright Blue), MSDC (Lavender), Start-Up Showcase (Burgundy), Visitors (Aloe).
